jokes.text: Lending? Makes it sound like I had a choice in the matter. I never should've agreed to this or let you know that I have a cabin or gotten to know any of you.
jokes.setup: Leslie thanks Ron for lending his cabin
jokes.punchline: Ron's escalating regret from agreeing to the plan to knowing anyone at all
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson character moment with excellent escalation structure - goes from reasonable complaint to existential regret about human connection

jokes.text: That's the spirit.
jokes.setup: Ron's increasingly misanthropic complaints
jokes.punchline: Leslie's cheerful mischaracterization of Ron's negativity
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective Leslie optimism playing against Ron's pessimism

jokes.text: I don't wanna over-hype it, but our parade makes the rose bowl parade look like a turds-on-wheels convention.
jokes.setup: Leslie building up Pawnee's founder's week parade
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous comparison involving 'turds-on-wheels convention'
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd Leslie hyperbole with memorable phrasing that captures her over-the-top civic pride

jokes.text: Easy: Most murders per capita. The guillotine was invented here. City Hall is run by the walrus mafia.
jokes.setup: Leslie asks April what makes Pawnee the best city
jokes.punchline: April lists increasingly dark and absurd 'positives' including walrus mafia
jokes.explanation: Perfect April deadpan subversion with escalating absurdity that only she could deliver - 'walrus mafia' is inspired

jokes.text: Cost of living is cheap. In about three years, I will have saved enough to pay off my condo in Seattle.
jokes.setup: Leslie asks Donna what makes Pawnee great
jokes.punchline: Donna reveals she's only there temporarily to save money for her real life elsewhere
jokes.explanation: Good character moment that subverts Leslie's hometown pride with Donna's practical detachment

jokes.text: You have a condo in Seattle?
jokes.setup: Donna casually mentions her Seattle condo
jokes.punchline: Leslie's shocked reaction to learning this about her colleague
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat that lands the surprise of Donna's revelation

jokes.text: Yes, I like the rain and the fish markets.
jokes.setup: Leslie's surprise about the Seattle condo
jokes.punchline: Donna's matter-of-fact explanation with oddly specific preferences
jokes.explanation: Nice Donna moment with unexpected specificity about fish markets adding character depth

jokes.text: I'm bored. Let's go have sex in a tree.
jokes.setup: Meeting discussing founder's week planning
jokes.punchline: Mona-Lisa's inappropriate suggestion delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mona-Lisa character moment - inappropriately sexual and weirdly specific location

jokes.text: He'll be back in eight minutes.
jokes.setup: Mona-Lisa and Jean-Ralphio leaving for tree sex
jokes.punchline: Tom's matter-of-fact timing prediction about their sexual encounter
jokes.explanation: Great uncomfortable humor - Tom's precise timing knowledge implies unfortunate familiarity

jokes.text: I don't really have any power in this relationship.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking why he brought Mona-Lisa
jokes.punchline: Tom's admission of complete powerlessness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tom's weakness in relationships through self-awareness

jokes.text: Anyone's welcome to join us in the tree!
jokes.setup: Jean-Ralphio and Mona-Lisa leaving for tree sex
jokes.punchline: Jean-Ralphio's enthusiastic invitation for group participation
jokes.explanation: Classic Jean-Ralphio inappropriate enthusiasm, though predictable for the character

jokes.text: Side note: I accidentally threw my sweat shirt away. That's why I was digging through the trash. Found that too. Pretty cool.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining finding the pregnancy test
jokes.punchline: His pride in finding his own discarded sweatshirt in the trash
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - finding his own trash is somehow an achievement worth celebrating

jokes.text: There's only one man for this job: Bert Macklin, FBI. They said I was retired. They said I was too dangerous for the Pawnee Police Department. Turns out they were right... and wrong.
jokes.setup: Andy deciding to investigate the pregnancy test mystery
jokes.punchline: Full Bert Macklin persona with dramatic backstory about being 'too dangerous'
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to running gag with new wrinkle about being 'too dangerous' - quintessential Andy delusion

jokes.text: Macklin, you son of a bitch.
jokes.setup: Andy in full Bert Macklin mode
jokes.punchline: Andy addressing himself in the third person
jokes.explanation: Nice capper to the Macklin bit with Andy's self-congratulatory third person address

jokes.text: Due to a tragic misunderstanding, the prettiest pig beauty pageant has been replaced by a pork rib barbecue competition.
jokes.setup: Announcements for founder's week festivities
jokes.punchline: The dark implication that the beauty pageant pigs became the barbecue
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy with perfect euphemistic phrasing - 'tragic misunderstanding' for what's clearly pig slaughter

jokes.text: Oh, no.
jokes.setup: Announcement about the pig pageant becoming barbecue
jokes.punchline: Leslie's horrified realization of what happened
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction that helps land the previous joke's dark implication

jokes.text: Pawnee raccoon attacks have decreased.
jokes.setup: Leslie listing civic improvements
jokes.punchline: Raccoon attacks being a measurable civic problem
jokes.explanation: Good worldbuilding humor establishing Pawnee's particular problems through casual mention

jokes.text: In fact, this forum is kind of my victory lap.
jokes.setup: Leslie feeling proud of her accomplishments
jokes.punchline: The dramatic irony of calling it a victory lap before everything goes wrong
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the irony to come - gains impact on rewatch when you know how badly it goes

jokes.text: Who'd like to start? Uh-oh.
jokes.setup: Leslie confidently asking for public input
jokes.punchline: Her immediate regret upon seeing who wants to speak
jokes.explanation: Effective moment showing Leslie's confidence immediately deflating

jokes.text: And frankly, I don't see any of those citizens standing up and complaining abo-- Okay. Now, I see them.
jokes.setup: Leslie arguing that citizens aren't complaining
jokes.punchline: Citizens literally standing up to complain as she speaks
jokes.explanation: Perfect physical comedy timing - Leslie's argument immediately contradicted by visual evidence

jokes.text: Whoa! Darren! Slow down! That sweat suit's not for sweatin' in, okay? This is crushed velvet. If you go at more than a brisk walk, it will fall apart.
jokes.setup: Tom running his clothing rental business
jokes.punchline: Sweat suit that can't handle sweating due to impractical fabric choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom logic - prioritizing style over function to absurd degree with crushed velvet sweat suit

jokes.text: I could add a baby section over there, call it 'Li'l Swaggers.' I could add some old people stuff, call it 'Rent-A-Sag.'
jokes.setup: Tom explaining business expansion plans
jokes.punchline: Wordplay names for different demographics, especially 'Rent-A-Sag' for elderly
jokes.explanation: Good Tom wordplay with escalation from cute to slightly mean, though puns can feel easy

jokes.text: Ugh, no. Old people are gross.
jokes.setup: Tom's business idea for elderly customers
jokes.punchline: Tom immediately abandoning the idea due to ageism
jokes.explanation: Simple character moment showing Tom's shallow priorities

jokes.text: Hey, dingdong! Can I go home? My tum-tum feels gross, and also, I hate working.
jokes.setup: Mona-Lisa as Tom's employee
jokes.punchline: Childish language and brutal honesty about work ethic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mona-Lisa - childish language ('tum-tum') combined with work-shirking honesty

jokes.text: Also, a lawyer guy called you. He sounded really sexy on the phone, and I was very into that. Like, I was, like, very into that.
jokes.setup: Mona-Lisa reporting a business call
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate oversharing about being attracted to lawyer's voice
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with repetitive phrasing emphasizing her inappropriate focus

jokes.text: All right, let's set up some boundaries on what's cool for you to say to me, okay?
jokes.setup: Mona-Lisa's inappropriate workplace comments
jokes.punchline: Tom's reasonable response to unreasonable behavior
jokes.explanation: Solid straight-man reaction setting up the next bit

jokes.text: Okay. I frenched my cousin one time.
jokes.setup: Tom asking for appropriate workplace boundaries
jokes.punchline: Mona-Lisa immediately sharing something deeply inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion - Tom asks for boundaries and Mona-Lisa immediately crosses new ones

jokes.text: I might still be in love with him.
jokes.setup: Already inappropriate confession about cousin
jokes.punchline: Making it even worse with ongoing romantic feelings
jokes.explanation: Great escalation making bad confession even worse - perfect Mona-Lisa boundary crossing

jokes.text: Babyface heard my demo? I will accept a record deal.
jokes.setup: Lawyer mentioning a client interested in his business
jokes.punchline: Tom's immediate assumption it's about his music career
jokes.explanation: Good Tom character moment showing his delusional optimism about his various ventures

jokes.text: Whoa, Diddy wants to buy Rent-A-Swag?
jokes.setup: Discussion about mysterious business buyer
jokes.punchline: Tom's immediate assumption it's a famous rapper
jokes.explanation: Sets up running gag of Tom assuming celebrity involvement

jokes.text: It's not Diddy.
jokes.setup: Tom's excited Diddy assumptions
jokes.punchline: Lawyer's flat denial
jokes.explanation: Simple deflation of Tom's excitement, economical delivery

jokes.text: Oh. Oh, hello...Ann.
jokes.setup: Andy in Bert Macklin mode encountering Ann
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt at casual greeting while playing detective
jokes.explanation: Good awkward Andy moment trying to be casual while obviously investigating

jokes.text: A little something they taught us down at Quantico.
jokes.setup: Ann asking what Andy wants
jokes.punchline: Andy claiming FBI training for basic observation
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of Macklin persona with specific FBI training reference

jokes.text: It's an accusation, 'cause I know your secret. You're pregnant.
jokes.setup: Andy saying 'Congratulations' to Ann
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was an accusation rather than congratulations
jokes.explanation: Nice subversion of expected congratulations into interrogation technique

jokes.text: You're not? Damn it. Who is?
jokes.setup: Ann denying pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Andy's frustrated detective work being derailed
jokes.explanation: Simple frustrated detective moment, economical delivery

jokes.text: I'm trying to get pregnant. That's not fair. I mean, great, and happy for them, and congratulations, whatever, but ooh! No! Boo!
jokes.setup: Learning someone else is pregnant
jokes.punchline: Ann's jealousy breaking through her attempt to be gracious
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ann's attempt at politeness failing under jealousy

jokes.text: Whoa, I don't think so. Bert Macklin works alone.
jokes.setup: Ann wanting to help investigate
jokes.punchline: Andy staying in character as lone wolf FBI agent
jokes.explanation: Solid continuation of Macklin character with lone wolf trope

jokes.text: Welcome to the FBI.
jokes.setup: Ann bribing Andy with burgers
jokes.punchline: Andy immediately abandoning his 'works alone' principle for food
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - instantly compromising his detective principles for burgers

jokes.text: Together, as a town, we lost an amount of weight equal to 800 pregnant manatees.
jokes.setup: Ben defending Leslie's health initiatives with statistics
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific measurement unit of pregnant manatees
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd measurement that feels both official and ridiculous - great Ben deadpan delivery

jokes.text: You somehow convinced the school board that napkins are a vegetable.
jokes.setup: Argument about school lunch programs
jokes.punchline: Absurd bureaucratic classification of napkins as vegetables
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist bureaucracy joke satirizing real-world school lunch controversies

jokes.text: Ever since you stripped us of our freedoms by making us hire women, we've had to turn one of our bathrooms into a woman's bathroom. We only have one bathroom now.
jokes.setup: Dewey complaining about gender equality requirements
jokes.punchline: Framing basic workplace equality as freedom-stripping tyranny
jokes.explanation: Good satire of workplace gender equality resistance with absurd framing

jokes.text: I'm so sorry, Dewey. That sounds like a horrible price to pay for something as trivial as gender equality.
jokes.setup: Dewey's complaint about bathroom equality
jokes.punchline: Leslie's perfectly sarcastic response calling gender equality 'trivial'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie sarcasm - cutting response that exposes the absurdity of the complaint

jokes.text: So you agree.
jokes.setup: Leslie's sarcastic response about gender equality
jokes.punchline: Dewey completely missing the sarcasm
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Dewey's complete obliviousness to obvious sarcasm

jokes.text: Now, if I wanna start my day off with a triple-decker pancake breakfast pizza, I gotta go to the one that's 10 minutes away, and that makes me 20 minutes late to work every day. I work at home.
jokes.setup: Bjorn complaining about inconvenience caused by park instead of Paunch Burger
jokes.punchline: Revealing he works from home, making the commute complaint absurd
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect reveal that undermines his entire complaint structure

jokes.text: Have you ever considered not eating that for breakfast?
jokes.setup: Bjorn's breakfast of triple-decker pancake breakfast pizza
jokes.punchline: Leslie's reasonable but futile suggestion
jokes.explanation: Simple straight-person response highlighting the absurdity

jokes.text: How many people am I talking to, Donna? One or 1 1/9?
jokes.setup: Andy investigating pregnancy in Bert Macklin mode
jokes.punchline: Bizarre mathematical reference to possible pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Creative way for Andy to ask about pregnancy while maintaining detective persona

jokes.text: Oh. Interesting. I don't remember saying anything about anyone being pregnant.
jokes.setup: Donna asking if he's asking about pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Andy's clumsy attempt to play innocent after obvious question
jokes.explanation: Good Andy detective incompetence - terrible at being subtle

jokes.text: Whew. This case just got interesting. Well, not just. It was pretty interesting to begin with. This case just remained interesting.
jokes.setup: Andy making dramatic detective pronouncement
jokes.punchline: Andy correcting himself and making it less dramatic
jokes.explanation: Nice self-aware correction showing Andy's attempt at dramatic flair falling apart

jokes.text: And now, this town a horrifying sex den where people can put their body parts anywhere they want to with impunity.
jokes.setup: Citizen complaining about town's moral decline
jokes.punchline: Overwrought description of basic sexual freedom
jokes.explanation: Good satire of moral panic with hilariously overwrought language

jokes.text: And deviant behavior in Pawnee is up a billion percent!
jokes.setup: Already hyperbolic complaint about moral decline
jokes.punchline: Mathematically impossible statistic
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with absurd statistics typical of moral panic rhetoric

jokes.text: This town is going to hell in a Gucci knock-off handbag, girl.
jokes.setup: Moral panic complaint
jokes.punchline: Updated version of 'going to hell in a handbasket' with fashion specificity
jokes.explanation: Creative update of classic phrase with character-appropriate fashion detail

jokes.text: Oh, I run a pornographic DVD emporium. Well, it used to be a quaint, little movie rental place with a lot of community value. Oh, it's much better now.
jokes.setup: Dennis saying Leslie helped his business
jokes.punchline: Revealing his business is pornography, which Leslie accidentally helped
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that puts Leslie in extremely awkward position publicly

jokes.text: I'm Brandi Maxxxx... Star of such films as the incredible Burt Wonderbone, A Good Guy to Lay Hard, and Argo.
jokes.setup: Brandi introducing herself and her films
jokes.punchline: Porn parody titles mixed with actual movie Argo
jokes.explanation: Good porn parody titles with unexpected legitimate film credit adding to the absurdity

jokes.text: I had a small part in Argo as well as the porn version Our Goo.
jokes.setup: Mentioning Argo among her credits
jokes.punchline: Clarifying she was in both the real and porn versions
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and clever porn title pun on Argo - 'Our Goo' is perfectly crude

jokes.text: I've seen the first 90 seconds. It's thrilling.
jokes.setup: Brandi mentioning her films
jokes.punchline: Tom's admission about watching porn with specific time reference
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with implication about Tom's viewing habits

jokes.text: Did you or did you not leave this positive pregnancy test in a dumpster in the woods?
jokes.setup: Andy investigating pregnancy mystery
jokes.punchline: Dramatically confronting Leslie publicly with evidence
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Andy's investigation reaching public confrontation

jokes.text: Someone-- I'm not saying Diddy, but I'm not not saying Diddy-- has offered to buy out Rent-A-Swag for a buttload of money.
jokes.setup: Tom announcing the business offer
jokes.punchline: Continuing his Diddy obsession with double negative logic
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of running gag with clever double negative phrasing

jokes.text: I'm talking pedicures on pedicures on pedicures.
jokes.setup: Tom describing treat-yourself possibilities
jokes.punchline: Absurd escalation of luxury pedicures
jokes.explanation: Good escalation in classic Tom luxury style, referencing treat yourself culture

jokes.text: I actually think that you should get a 'Hello Kitty' tattoo.
jokes.setup: Asked about the business decision
jokes.punchline: April giving completely irrelevant advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect April non-sequitur showing her disinterest in Tom's business concerns

jokes.text: Uh, burn it for the insurance money.
jokes.setup: Tom clarifying he wants advice about the store
jokes.punchline: April suggesting insurance fraud
jokes.explanation: Classic April dark humor suggesting criminal activity as business solution

jokes.text: God, nobody cares, Gary.
jokes.setup: Jerry trying to give advice
jokes.punchline: Tom's harsh dismissal and wrong name
jokes.explanation: Solid callback to Jerry mistreatment with wrong name usage

jokes.text: I miss you guys.
jokes.setup: Being dismissed and insulted by Tom
jokes.punchline: Jerry's pathetically positive response to mistreatment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry moment - responding to abuse with genuine affection

jokes.text: Parades and your butt: My two vices.
jokes.setup: Ben mentioning Leslie's love of parades
jokes.punchline: Leslie's unexpectedly sexual response
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leslie's surprising directness about attraction

jokes.text: We always use protection... but I'm pretty positive she pokes holes in them.
jokes.setup: Andy asking if Mona-Lisa could be pregnant
jokes.punchline: Tom's disturbing revelation about sabotaged contraception
jokes.explanation: Dark relationship humor showing Tom's awareness of manipulation but helplessness

jokes.text: It's called 'Womb There It Is.' That's her brand.
jokes.setup: Andy asking about the pregnancy test brand
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'Whoomp! There It Is' for pregnancy test name
jokes.explanation: Excellent pun that feels both ridiculous and plausible as actual product name

jokes.text: I'm gonna have a baby with Jean-Ralphio's sister? Oh, God. What have I done? Like, to humanity?
jokes.setup: Tom thinking he's having a baby with Mona-Lisa
jokes.punchline: Escalating from personal concern to species-level guilt
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Tom's horror expanding from personal to cosmic implications

jokes.text: I'm the Nipple King.
jokes.setup: Chris needing to talk to Leslie urgently
jokes.punchline: Revealing his ceremonial rubber nipple title
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Chris moment - delivered with complete sincerity making it even funnier

jokes.text: No fun for you!
jokes.setup: Anti-Leslie parade float
jokes.punchline: Reference to Soup Nazi with anti-Leslie sentiment
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference adapted for anti-Leslie sentiment

jokes.text: This float represents a group of over 300 business owners, individuals, and community leaders who are protesting unfair government regulation. We are the committee to recall Leslie Knope.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to defend herself at the parade
jokes.punchline: Revealing organized political opposition in parade form
jokes.explanation: Good escalation revealing the true threat to Leslie's career

jokes.text: Yeah. It's mine.
jokes.setup: Dramatic buildup about pregnancy test ownership
jokes.punchline: Simple admission after all the investigation
jokes.explanation: Effective misdirection payoff after Andy's elaborate investigation

jokes.text: Psych! Ah, ha, ha. Look at your stupid face. You had a dumdum face.
jokes.setup: Tom's emotional response to pregnancy news
jokes.punchline: Mona-Lisa revealing it was a cruel joke
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mona-Lisa cruelty with childish language making it even more cutting

jokes.text: Do you know how many different pills I take, birth control or otherwise? There is no way that I could be pregnant with a human baby.
jokes.setup: Explaining why she can't be pregnant
jokes.punchline: Specifying 'human baby' implying other possibilities
jokes.explanation: Great dark humor with 'human baby' specification suggesting alien possibilities

jokes.text: Tommy has his life back! Let's go!
jokes.setup: Learning Mona-Lisa isn't pregnant
jokes.punchline: Tom's celebration of avoiding parenthood with her
jokes.explanation: Good Tom relief moment showing his priorities

jokes.text: Nah! I'm just kidding, man. She's the worst. You dodged a bullet, baby.
jokes.setup: Tom celebrating avoiding pregnancy with Mona-Lisa
jokes.punchline: Jean-Ralphio agreeing his own sister is terrible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jean-Ralphio moment casually insulting his sister while celebrating Tom's relief

jokes.text: Besides, the math doesn't add up because we only have sex every single day.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining why April can't be pregnant
jokes.punchline: Andy's illogical reasoning about daily sex preventing pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - completely backwards understanding of reproduction

jokes.text: When we went to Hawaii, you woke me up at 3:00 A.M. to watch Meet the Press.
jokes.setup: Ben suggesting they move to the Bahamas and fish
jokes.punchline: Ben pointing out Leslie's work obsession would prevent relaxation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leslie's inability to relax even on vacation

jokes.text: Step up. Step Up 2: The Streets. I'm sorry, my adrenaline is pumping, so all I can think of is dance movie titles. Stomp the Yard. There's one.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to deliver defiant political speech
jokes.punchline: Her brain fixating on dance movies due to adrenaline
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Leslie's stress manifesting in unexpected ways

jokes.text: You Got Served. Silver Linings Playbook, kind of.
jokes.setup: Leslie's dance movie fixation continuing
jokes.punchline: Adding questionable dance movie and admitting the stretch
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with 'kind of' acknowledgment that Silver Linings isn't really a dance movie

jokes.text: I haven't had a date in a while, but I'm learning a lot about me.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking if people are better off
jokes.punchline: Reporter's personal overshare during professional coverage
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment with reporter crossing professional boundaries

jokes.text: So, in conclusion, recall, shmecall. No, I can't end my big press conference by saying 'Recall, shmecall.' That's ridiculous.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to end her press conference dramatically
jokes.punchline: Self-aware rejection of her own weak closing line
jokes.explanation: Great self-aware moment showing Leslie's standards even under pressure

jokes.text: Last one to pick up 100 rusty cans has still accomplished a great deal.
jokes.setup: Chris motivating volunteers for river cleanup
jokes.punchline: Subverting competitive motivation with participation trophy mentality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris positivity turning competition into universal encouragement

jokes.text: Sex is ridiculous.
jokes.setup: Discussing how things are going with Chris
jokes.punchline: Ann's blunt assessment of their physical relationship
jokes.explanation: Good Ann directness about physical relationship with economical delivery

jokes.text: I wanna wait until we're 50 and then adopt a set of creepy adult twins from Romania.
jokes.setup: Andy asking about having kids
jokes.punchline: April's disturbing and specific alternative to normal parenthood
jokes.explanation: Absolutely perfect April dark humor - specific, disturbing, and completely in character

jokes.text: It's called capitalism, Mr. Haverford, and you are about to get a free lesson. Only this free lesson is going to cost you.
jokes.setup: Tom complaining about competitor using his ideas
jokes.punchline: Self-contradictory threat about free lesson that costs
jokes.explanation: Good corporate villain wordplay with self-aware contradiction

jokes.text: So it's not free?
jokes.setup: Trevor's contradictory threat
jokes.punchline: Tom's literal-minded confusion about the logic
jokes.explanation: Good Tom moment showing his confusion at contradictory corporate speak

jokes.text: This sucks, but I have to admit: It's a pretty baller move.
jokes.setup: Learning about the business competition
jokes.punchline: Tom's grudging respect for being outmaneuvered
jokes.explanation: Good Tom character moment showing his appreciation for business strategy even when it hurts him

jokes.text: It is Diddy.
jokes.setup: Tom's continued speculation about Diddy involvement
jokes.punchline: Lawyer finally confirming it
jokes.explanation: Payoff to running gag that validates Tom's celebrity assumptions

jokes.text: It's not Diddy.
jokes.setup: Just confirmed it was Diddy
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting himself again
jokes.explanation: Continues the running confusion keeping the mystery alive

jokes.text: If I'm the same as I was a year ago, I'm happy.
jokes.setup: Discussion about being better off than a year ago
jokes.punchline: Ron's contentment with complete lack of change
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron philosophy - happiness through stasis rather than progress
